name: scheduling
description: Generic scheduling primitive — public booking + admin CRUD. Google Calendar is a soft, opt-in dependency.

rules.description: List active services (public, anonymous-allowed). Sorted by sort_order asc.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_services
pipelineConfig.description: List active scheduling services for the public booking page

rules.description: List active resources optionally filtered by service_id (M:N via scheduling_resource_service). Public, anonymous-allowed.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_resources
pipelineConfig.description: List resources that can perform a given service (or all active if no service_id)

rules.description: Compute available booking slots for service+resource over [from, to]. DB-as-truth slot math. NOTE: google_calendar:freebusy augmentation deferred — MCP tool enum doesn't yet include the new handler. Will be added once MCP catches up; function_handler already handles missing googleStep gracefully.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_availability
pipelineConfig.description: Returns available slot start times for a service. If resource_id is provided, restricts to that resource; otherwise fans out across all eligible resources for the service and dedupes slots by start time (first by sort_order wins). Slot.end = start + service.duration. No buffer logic — bookings only block their own [starts_at, ends_at] range.

rules.description: Create a booking. Re-checks slot availability before write to avoid race-induced double-booking. DB-only for now; google_calendar:create_event mirror pending MCP enum update.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_booking_create
pipelineConfig.description: Create a confirmed booking. Accepts resource_id (specific stylist) or null (Any stylist mode). In Any mode, picks the first eligible resource (lowest sort_order) that's still free at [starts_at, ends_at] and writes that real resource id. Captures user.id when authenticated; anonymous bookings store user_id = null.

config.body: {"error": "SLOT_TAKEN", "message": "That slot was just taken — please pick another."}

rules.description: Cancel or reschedule a booking via opaque reschedule_token. Token = the auth credential (per design decisions §7).

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_booking_manage
pipelineConfig.description: Token-gated cancel + reschedule. Mirrors to Google Calendar (delete/update) when the booking has a google_event_id from the original create.

rules.description: Admin: list all services (active and inactive). auth_required + admin role.
Admin: list all resources (active and inactive).
Admin: list all resource-service M:N links.
Admin: list all working-hours rows (per-resource and site-wide).
Admin: list all time-off rows.
Admin: create a service.
Admin: create a resource (staff/room/equipment).
Admin: create a resource-service M:N link.
Admin: create a working-hours row.
Admin: create a time-off row (vacation/holiday).
Admin: partial-update a service. Read-then-merge to preserve unspecified fields.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_admin_update_service
pipelineConfig.description: Update a scheduling_service. Overlays only the fields the client sent on top of the existing record. Responds with the merged record.

rules.description: Admin: partial-update a resource. Read-then-merge.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_admin_update_resource
pipelineConfig.description: Update a scheduling_resource. Reads existing row and overlays only the fields the client actually sent (via request.body.hasOwnProperty), so a PATCH that names a single field doesn't blank the rest. Responds with the merged record.

rules.description: Admin: partial-update a working-hours row.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_admin_update_working_hours
pipelineConfig.description: Update a scheduling_working_hours row. Overlays only the fields the client sent.

rules.description: Admin: partial-update a time-off row.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_admin_update_time_off
pipelineConfig.description: Update a scheduling_time_off row. Overlays only the fields the client sent.

rules.description: Admin: hard-delete a service. Prefer soft-delete via PATCH active=false in the UI.
Admin: hard-delete a resource. Prefer soft-delete (active=false) for resources with bookings.
Admin: delete a resource-service link.
Admin: delete a working-hours row.
Admin: delete a time-off row.
Admin: read the singleton scheduling settings record.
Admin: partial-update the singleton scheduling settings. labels is a json field so we skip form_handler and merge directly from request.body.
Returns whether the signed-in user is a scheduling admin, and total admin count for bootstrap UX.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_admin_me
pipelineConfig.description: Returns { isAdmin, totalAdmins } for the signed-in SuperTokens session. totalAdmins lets the UI surface a 'claim ownership' CTA when 0.

rules.description: Claim scheduling-admin ownership while the table is empty. Locks after first claim.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_admin_claim
pipelineConfig.description: First-claim bootstrap. While scheduling_admin_user is empty, the signed-in caller is added as the first admin. Subsequent calls 403. Boolean conditions are precomputed in a function_handler since pipeline conditions don't evaluate inline === comparisons.

config.body: {"success": true, "isAdmin": true, "message": "You are the scheduling admin."}
{"success": false, "isAdmin": false, "message": "Scheduling admin already claimed by another user."}

rules.description: Lists Google sub-calendars accessible via the project's CE-stored Google Calendar integration. Used by the admin scheduling page to populate the per-stylist calendar dropdown.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_admin_google_calendars
pipelineConfig.description: List Google sub-calendars from the project's connected Google account. Returns { calendars: [{id, summary, primary}], email, skipped } in the shape the useGoogleCalendarConnect hook expects. optional: true on the google_calendar step so a missing integration returns skipped:true with an empty list, not an error.

rules.description: Returns confirmed bookings for the signed-in user, denormalized with service + resource names so the client doesn't need a second round trip.

pipelineConfig.name: scheduling_my_bookings
pipelineConfig.description: List the signed-in user's confirmed bookings (upcoming + past), ordered by starts_at asc, with service.name + resource.name + a manage URL inlined so the frontend can render directly without further queries.
